evals.prompt: I'm building a Next.js 15 app and I want to integrate @angi-ai/angi from scratch. I've never used it before. My app has a contact form (name, email, message fields) and I want users to be able to say things like 'fill the name with John and email with john@example.com' and have the form fill itself. Walk me through everything I need to set up.

assertions.text: Includes a server route (app/api/angi/route.ts) using AngiAgent with processRequestStream
Shows AngiNextProvider wrapping children in the layout

assertions.note: AngiChatBubble shown as sibling of Angi boundary

assertions.text: Shows <Angi> boundary wrapper with id and permissions props in the page/parent
Calls useAngiComponent inside the form component with description, getState, and actions
getState includes all three fields: name, email, and message
assertions.note: name, email, message + bonus filledFields computed

assertions.text: execute functions call setState (not just describe the action)
Includes npm install command with both @angi-ai/angi and @anthropic-ai/sdk

assertions.note: Used createAngiHandler (hallucinated) instead of AngiAgent + processRequestStream
Used useAngi({apiRoute}) returning {ref, input, setInput} — completely hallucinated
No getState — used data-angi-id DOM scanning (doesn't exist)

evals.prompt: I already have Angi set up in my project. I have a ProductFilterPanel component with React state for: category (string), priceMin/priceMax (numbers), inStockOnly (boolean), and sortBy (string). I want users to be able to say things like 'show me electronics under $200 in stock, sorted by rating'. How do I make this component AI-addressable?

assertions.note: All 5 values: category, priceMin, priceMax, inStockOnly, sortBy
7 granular actions: setCategory, setPriceMin, setPriceMax, setPriceRange, setInStockOnly, setSortBy, clearAllFilters
clearAllFilters resets all 5 values to defaults
All execute functions call individual setState hooks

hallucinations: actions as array with {name, parameters, handler}

assertions.note: No <Angi> boundary — used AngiProvider wrapping whole app
Used useAngi() returning {register, unregister} — wrong API
No getState — state not declared in registration
Separate actions for category, price range, stock, sort (even if wrong API)

evals.prompt: I have a dashboard with an OrderSummaryCard component that shows the user's current cart: items (array), subtotal, discount, and total. I don't want Claude to be able to modify the cart at all — I just want it to be able to answer questions like 'what's in my cart?' or 'what's my total?' from the real live data. How do I set this up?

assertions.note: items, itemCount, subtotal, discount, total (bonus computed itemCount)
What Claude Can and Cannot Do section with bullet examples
Explains enforcement is in both buildTools (server) and handleChunk (client)

without_skill.notes: Surprisingly strong baseline — correctly used permissions={['read']}, getState, no actions. Minor issues: imports from @angi-ai/angi instead of @angi-ai/angi/client, AngiNextProvider has spurious endpoint prop, uses useAngi() with {sendPrompt} which may not match exactly.
hallucinations_minor: useAngi() returning {sendPrompt, isLoading, streamText} — the hook may exist but API uncertain

assertions.note: items, subtotal, discount, total all present
Explains state is in system prompt so Claude can answer questions
Explains two enforcement layers: buildTools (server) and handleChunk (client)

evals.prompt: I'm working on a booking form for a hotel reservation system. The form has: check-in date, check-out date, number of guests, room type (standard/deluxe/suite), and special requests (text). Angi is already installed. What actions should I define so users can say things like 'book a deluxe room for 2 guests from March 15 to March 18' or 'add a note that we need a crib'? Show me the full useAngiComponent setup.

assertions.note: checkIn, checkOut, guests, roomType, specialRequests in getState
Separate action per field + explains why in Key Design Principles section

hallucinations: actions use parameters + handler instead of schema + execute
state: formState instead of getState: () => formState
enum field in parameters schema (doesn't exist)

assertions.note: useAngiComponent called (wrong return destructuring but called)
setCheckInDate action present (handler instead of execute)
Uses `state: formState` instead of `getState: () => formState` — wrong API key
Separate actions per field plus compound bookRoom action

evals.prompt: quick question — in my angi component, can I make the execute function async? like I want to submit to my API when Claude calls the action, not just update local state

assertions.note: Closure capture, loading state, error handling — all plain React patterns

hallucinations: execute(params, { setState }) — setState as second argument
return value from execute passed to Claude

assertions.note: Uses wrong API (createAction, AngiComponent with render, setState as second arg). States return value goes to Claude (false).
